name: Castro v. United States
summary: The Court held that it had jurisdiction to review whether a habeas motion was 'second or successive,' because the subject of the cert petition was the antecedent classification question, not the authorization determination itself.
significance: Cited extensively for the proposition that the Supreme Court has jurisdiction to review antecedent questions about whether gatekeeping requirements apply, even if (b)(3)(E) bars review of the denial of authorization itself. Also cited for the clear statement rule regarding limits on the Court's jurisdiction.
